L1.area: Stream multiplexing flow control and window management

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the stream multiplexer's send-window accounting. A remote peer can send a specially crafted window update frame with a very large credit value that causes arithmetic overflow in the per-stream window state, triggering a panic in the connection state machine. This results in a remotely exploitable denial of service that does not require authentication.

L3.area: Stream multiplexing flow control and window management
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the stream multiplexer's send-window accounting. A remote peer can send a specially crafted window update frame with a very large credit value that causes arithmetic overflow in the per-stream window state, triggering a panic in the connection state machine. This results in a remotely exploitable denial of service that does not require authentication.

ground_truth.reason: The send-window credit accounting uses unchecked u32 addition when applying WindowUpdate frames from the remote peer. A malicious peer can send a WindowUpdate with a large credit value that overflows the u32 send window, causing an arithmetic panic and crashing the connection.
